div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting: Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there, the ploughgate of land and ten acres in the territory of Tranent, towards Falside, that Robert de Quincy has given to them for the exchange of the ploughgate of land at Preston that the same canons claimed against the same Robert and the monks of Newbattle. For them to hold in free and pure and perpetual alms with free exit and the common pasture of Tranent, as much as belongs to such a land, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as the charter of the said Robert bears witness, reserving my service. As witness, Richard de Prebenda my clerk, Robert de Quincy, Philip de Mowbray, William of Lindsay, William de Vaux, Herbert de Camera, Philip of Lundin, Walter Murdoch. At Haddington on the 19th day of August.
